D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Cyanine Dye Length — Particle-in-a-Box

a symmetric cyanine has 2k+1 methine carbons between the two nitrogens; the free-electron-in-a-box gap shrinks like 1/N², and the measured λ_max jumps ~100 nm per extra methine

A cyanine R₂N⁺=CH–(CH=CH)ₖ–NR₂ is a quintessential textbook molecule: the π electrons sit in a 1-D box of length L ≈ 1.39 Å × (2k+2). The Kuhn free-electron formula puts λ_max ≈ 224 + 71(n−1) nm where n is the number of methine carbons. Each extra –CH=CH– unit shifts the absorption ~100 nm to the red.

Pick the methine count via the slider. Measured λ_max values come from Förster & Brode 1934. The visible-spectrum band shows where the absorption lands; the swatch is the perceived (complementary) colour.
